description: Single-zero asymmetric displacement of zeta zero closest to T0=85.6993, with beta=0.3085, sigma=2.0, mimicking L_DH geometric parameters from r28. Tests whether beta/sigma ratio (not intrinsic L_DH structure) controls Weil-form growth exponent.

parameters.basis: h_i(z) = exp(-(z-T0)^2/(2 sigma^2)) * (z-T0)^i (Gaussian-modulated monomials, no factorial)
parameters.M_zeros_formula: Re[v v^T] with v = h(±gamma - i*beta) (non-Hermitian, functional-equation pair)
parameters.M_arith_formula: sum (log p)/sqrt(p^k) * [h(k log p) h(k log p)^T + h(-k log p) h(-k log p)^T] for p^k <= prime_bound
parameters.note_M_arith: M_arith is numerically zero (~10^-661 trace) because Gaussian centered at T0=85.7 with sigma=2.0 vanishes at k log p << T0; arithmetic side does not contribute appreciably.

conclusion: Single-zero asymmetric displacement of the closest zeta zero (gamma_23≈84.7355) at beta=0.3085 with basis centered at T0=85.6993, sigma=2.0 produces |lambda_min| growing with power-law exponent alpha = 3.76 (J=8..20). This is well above the hypothesized alpha≈1 of L_DH (r28: alpha=0.90) but well below the small-delta single-shift exponent (r51: alpha=13.98). Hypothesis NOT supported: matching beta/sigma ratio alone does not reproduce L_DH's weak signal. The growth ratio (J=8->20) is 26.8x vs 2.2x for L_DH, ~12x larger growth despite identical geometric parameters.
